info.name: Banyan API
info.description:
Complete collection for the Banyan taxonomy platform REST API.

Collection variable `base_url` defaults to http://localhost:8000/api/v1.

The '00 Happy Path' folder runs top-to-bottom and auto-captures graph_id, node_id_root, node_id_child, and link_id into collection variables via test scripts.

item.name: 00 Happy Path
item.description: Run this folder top-to-bottom for an end-to-end walkthrough. IDs are captured automatically.

item.name: 13 Batch — add node + link in one transaction

exec: pm.test('name is Restored Graph', () => pm.expect(r.name).to.equal('Restored Graph'));

request.description: Restore a snapshot as a new graph. The original graph is untouched. Returns the new graph dict.

item.name: BQL Queries
item.description:
POST /api/v1/query — Banyan Query Language examples. Two sub-folders:

• Toy Queries — pattern demonstrations against the Product Taxonomy graph built by 00 Happy Path. graph_id must be set in collection variables.

• Gravity Queries — real-world crosswalk and traversal queries against the live Gravity SDOH / Open Eligibility / SNOMED CT SDOH / ICD-10-CM SDOH graphs. Requires ingest utilities to have been run against the dev database.

item.name: Toy Queries
item.description: Six pattern demonstrations using the Product Taxonomy graph from 00 Happy Path. Run that folder first so graph_id, node_id_root, and node_id_child are set.

item.name: 01 Full graph export (no starting, no steps)
item.description: Implicit seed = root node, implicit default step = FROM all types depth 50. Returns every node in the graph. Equivalent to GET /export.

item.name: 02 Subtree from a named node
item.description: Seed = Electronics node (created by Happy Path step 02). Default FROM traversal returns the full subtree rooted there.

item.name: 03 Immediate children only (depth 1)
item.description: One hop FROM the root via HIERARCHICAL links only. Returns direct children; excludes the root itself.

item.name: 04 Ancestor chain (TO direction)
item.description: Walk inbound HIERARCHICAL links from Smartphones upward. Returns the full ancestor chain in hop order (closest parent first). Each result item contains the traversed link and the ancestor node.

item.name: 05 Sibling nodes (zig-zag)
item.description: Step 1: walk TO the parent (collect=false — used as waypoint only). Step 2: walk FROM the parent back down, reaching all siblings. The seed (Smartphones) is pre-added to the dedup set so it does not re-appear in step 2. Result: seed + siblings; parent never collected.

item.name: 06 Compound AND predicate with metadata filter
item.description: Seed nodes must satisfy both: name contains electronics (case-insensitive) AND metadata.department_code == EL. Updated by Happy Path step 07 to department_code EL. Demonstrates compound AND and metadata path predicates.

item.name: Gravity Queries
item.description: Twelve queries against the live Gravity SDOH / OE / SNOMED CT / ICD-10-CM graphs. Graph names used verbatim — they must match the ingest-generated names in the database.

item.name: 01 All Gravity L1 social-risk domains
item.description: Returns all 28 top-level SDOH domains (Food Insecurity, Housing Instability, Elder Abuse, etc.). One hop from root.

item.name: 02 Full domain subtree — Food Insecurity (L1 + 8 L2 activities)
item.description: Seed = Food Insecurity (L1 domain). Default FROM traversal returns the domain and all its L2 activity nodes (Diagnoses, Goals, Procedures, Screening Assessments, etc.).

item.name: 03 L2 activities for Housing Instability (one hop)
item.description: Returns only the immediate L2 value-set activity nodes under Housing Instability. depth: 1 prevents descending further.

item.name: 04 Peer domains of Food Insecurity (zig-zag siblings)
item.description: Step 1 walks TO the Gravity root (collect=false). Step 2 walks FROM the root back to all L1 domains. Result: Food Insecurity (seed, step 0) + all 27 peer domains (step 2). The root is never collected.

item.name: 05 SNOMED codes for Food Insecurity (2-step crosswalk)
item.description: Two-step cross-graph traversal. Step 1: Food Insecurity → L2 activities (collect=false). Step 2: each L2 activity → its member SNOMED CT codes via HAS_MEMBER links. HAS_MEMBER! uses exact-type match (no family expansion).

item.name: 06 ICD-10-CM codes for Food Insecurity (2-step crosswalk)
item.description: Same pattern as query 05 but targeting the ICD-10-CM SDOH Slice graph. Returns Z-codes and other ICD-10 codes mapped to Food Insecurity value sets (e.g. Z59.41, E63.9).

item.name: 07 All clinical codes for Food Insecurity — SNOMED + ICD-10 in one query
item.description: Combines queries 05 and 06 into a single request by listing both clinical graphs in the step. LINK_NODE format includes the HAS_MEMBER link (with from/to graph IDs) alongside each code node, making it easy to identify which code system each result came from.

item.name: 08 Reverse lookup — Gravity activities referencing a SNOMED code
item.description: Starts in the SNOMED CT graph. Walks inbound HAS_MEMBER links (TO direction) to find which Gravity L2 activity nodes claim this code as a member. Answers: which Gravity value sets contain SNOMED code 73438004? Seed (the SNOMED node) is included for context.

item.name: 09 Open Eligibility — full taxonomy
item.description: Full export of the Open Eligibility taxonomy (~289 nodes). No starting = root seed; default FROM step traverses all 290 nodes; include_seed=false drops the $ sentinel. Use limit to page large results.

item.name: 10 Open Eligibility — Food branch subtree
item.description: Subtree rooted at the OE top-level Food category. Returns Food and all its descendant service/program types. Seed is included so the root of the subtree is visible in the result.

item.name: 11 ICD-10-CM Z59.x — housing and economic social determinants
item.description: Finds all ICD-10-CM codes in the Z59 block (Problems related to housing and economic circumstances: housing instability, homelessness, inadequate food, etc.). source_id_prefix matches the FHIR-canonical code URI. Default step traverses any outbound links from those nodes (likely none — leaf codes).

item.name: 12 Verbose multi-hop diagnostic — Housing Instability → activities → clinical codes
item.description: Full pipeline with verbose=true. Response envelope includes steps[] diagnostics: per-step input_count, output_count, collected, and truncated flags. Useful for debugging large multi-hop queries. Step 1 collects L2 activities (unlike queries 05-07 where they are skipped); step 2 appends clinical codes from both terminology graphs.

item.name: Error Cases
item.description: Requests that should return 4xx — useful for verifying error handling.
